title: Bruise-leech crawler
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Bruise-Leech1.jpg]] |name=Bruise-leech crawler |designation= |class=[[Annelid]]{{CSWECite|I|95|bruise-leech crawler}} |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Brown]]{{CW|23}} |haircolor=Black |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=*Circular [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] lined with [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] *2 protrusions behind head *Left behind blue trails |lifespan= |origin=[[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|He says this may hurt a bit.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, to Anakin Skywalker|}} '''Bruise-leech crawlers''', alternatively known as '''Nelvaan bruise-leech crawlers''', were [[Leech/Legends|leech]]-like creatures native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]]. [[Orvos]], [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] of [[Rokrul]] village on Nelvaan, covered [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's]] chest with bruise-leech crawlers as part of a ritual before sending him off to [[Battle of Nelvaan|destroy]] a nearby [[Techno Union laboratory]]. The leeches caused pain as they crawled over Skywalker's torso and left behind [[Color/Legends|blue]] trails. ==Biology and appearance== Native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]], bruise-[[Leech/Legends|leech]] crawlers, sometimes referred to as "Nelvaan bruise-leech crawlers,"{{InsiderCite|94|Jawa's Corner}} were [[annelid]]s that were reddish-brown in [[Color/Legends|color]], with some having little black patches of [[Hair/Legends|fur]] toward their back ends. They had a circular [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] that was lined with [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] and two protrusions directly behind the head. The crawlers were capable of leaving a blue trail behind them as they moved. ==Behavior== When placed on someone's body, the crawlers could make intricate rectangular designs out of the blue trails they left behind. ==History== [[File:Bruise-leech_on_anakin.png|thumb|right|200px|The crawlers leave a blue design on Anakin Skywalker's torso.]] {{Quote|Is this really necessary?''"
"''It's part of their ritual. You must be respectful.|Skywalker questions Kenobi over the necessity of the crawlers|}} In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' [[Orvos]], the [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] of the [[Nelvaanian/Legends|Nelvaanian]] [[City/Legends|village]] of [[Rokrul]], used bruise-leech crawlers in a ceremony to prepare the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] for his journey to [[Battle of Nelvaan|destroy]] a nearby [[Techno Union laboratory|laboratory]] operated by the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] that used a [[siphon generator]] which had plunged the world into an [[Water/Legends|ice]] age. The crawlers proceeded to create a rectangular design across Skywalker's torso with the blue trails they left behind as they moved slowly, causing the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] much pain. ==Behind the scenes== The bruise-leech crawlers made their first and only appearance in the [[Chapter 23 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|twenty-third chapter]] of the animated television microseries ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', which aired on [[March 23]], [[2005]].{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-xxiii-370295|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 2, Episode 3: Chapter XXIII}} The [[Species/Legends|species]] was then named in the Nelvaanian [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry.{{DB|species|nelvaanian|Nelvaanian}} ==Appearances== *{{CW|23}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|94|Jawa's Corner}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|species|nelvaanian|Nelvaanian}} {{1stID|bruise-leech crawler}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Bruise-Leech Crawler }} [[Category:Creatures of Nelvaan]] [[Category:Creatures of unspecified diet]] [[Category:Invertebrates]] [[Category:Species of undefined sentience]]
